Early Access
THE ADS ENGINEER

Your ad team doesn’t need new tools.
They need to operate like engineers.

Your own people, leveled up to direct the agents instead of doing the work.

THE ANALOGY

A software engineer doesn’t code manually anymore.

Software EngineerAds EngineerDIRECTS THE AGENTS THATship the code.run the campaigns.
The engineer stopped typing and started directing.The same move, now for advertising.
WHAT IT’S MADE OF

Five disciplines your people already have.

Held together by the one that’s new to the room.

The Ads Engineer.

Where five disciplines converge into one operator.

WHAT IT DOES

Build at the systems level.
Automate at the campaign level.

The work your team does by hand, rebuilt as systems that run themselves.

PIPELINE · MORNING PERFORMANCE REPORT06:00 daily · 2 min
01PULLSpend + results across 4 platforms1,284 rows✓
02RECONCILECollapsed to one schema · “Meta 4× ROAS vs revenue” resolved0 mismatches
03CHECK PACINGActuals vs the media plan, campaign by campaign
04ASSEMBLEQ3 daily report built · tables, deltas, commentary
05DELIVERPosted to #performance · emailed the client
◐You review the 2 flagged exceptions.Everything else shipped before standup. No one touched it.HUMAN EDGE
THE DISTINCTION
THE PERFORMANCE MARKETER

Optimizes campaigns.

Help that ends when you close the tab.

THE ADS ENGINEER

Builds the systems that act without waiting for a human to notice.

Leverage that runs without you. The systems stay, and compound.

Most teams have the first. The second is what’s next.

ALREADY REAL

The role exists. It’s been missing a name.

$132K–$198K

Stripe formalized a forward-deployed AI role inside marketing, to build the systems and own the infrastructure.

$100M+ SPEND

Agencies running hundreds of millions are moving their best ops people into this function.

NO NAME YET

The practitioners are already doing the work. The title just hasn’t caught up.

BECOME IT

This is who your team becomes. We’re how it gets there.

Zyou builds the systems. Forge embeds and trains your team to run them.
